text: The Stoics divided the virtues into four: wisdom, justice, courage, and temperance — and held that they were unified. To possess any one fully is to possess all four; to lack any one is to lack virtue altogether. This doctrine of the unity of virtue had a sharp implication for attention: a person who allows their mind to be endlessly scattered by trivial pursuits is not merely inefficient but is, in a precise sense, failing at justice and wisdom simultaneously. Scattered attention is not a productivity problem to be optimized — it is a symptom of a soul that has not yet decided what it values.
question: According to the passage, what does scattered attention indicate for the Stoics?

options.text: A need for better cognitive tools and scheduling practices
A soul that has not yet decided what it values
A surplus of creative energy seeking an outlet
An imbalance between courage and temperance specifically
